name: OpenAI
comment:
This package is NOT intended to be run on the browser since `apiKey` will be exposed. Call it from a server like [elm-webapp](https://github.com/choonkeat/elm-webapp) or [lamdera.com](https://lamdera.com) instead.

    OpenAI.Edits.createEdits input
        |> OpenAI.withConfig cfg
        |> Http.task

See <https://beta.openai.com/docs/api-reference/introduction>

@docs Config, withConfig

aliases.name: Config
aliases.type: { organizationId : String.String, apiKey : String.String, baseUrl : Maybe.Maybe String.String }

values.name: withConfig
values.comment:
<https://platform.openai.com/docs/api-reference/making-requests>

Add necessary headers to authorize a http request to OpenAI. This is a necessary step before calling [`Http.task`](https://package.elm-lang.org/packages/elm/http/latest/Http#task).

    create
        { model = OpenAI.ModelID.TextEmbeddingAda002
        , input = "The food was delicious and the waiter..."
        , user = Nothing
        }
        |> OpenAI.withConfig cfg
        |> Http.task
values.type: OpenAI.Config -> Ext.Http.TaskInput x a -> Ext.Http.TaskInput x a

name: OpenAI.Audio
comment:
<https://platform.openai.com/docs/api-reference/audio>

@docs createTranscription, createTranslation

@docs Model, Output, ResponseFormat, TranscriptionInput, TranslationInput

aliases.name: Output
aliases.type: { text : String.String }

aliases.name: TranscriptionInput
aliases.comment:
`file`: The the audio file to transcribe, in one of these formats: mp3, mp4, mpeg, mpga, m4a, wav, or webm.

`prompt`: An optional text to guide the model's style or continue a previous audio segment. The prompt should match the audio language.

`language`: The language of the input audio. Supplying the input language in [ISO-639-1 format](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_ISO_639-1_codes) will improve accuracy and latency.
aliases.type: { file : String.String, model : OpenAI.Audio.Model, prompt : Maybe.Maybe String.String, response_format : Maybe.Maybe OpenAI.Audio.ResponseFormat, temperature : Maybe.Maybe Basics.Float, language : Maybe.Maybe String.String }

aliases.name: TranslationInput
aliases.type: { file : String.String, model : OpenAI.Audio.Model, prompt : Maybe.Maybe String.String, response_format : Maybe.Maybe OpenAI.Audio.ResponseFormat, temperature : Maybe.Maybe Basics.Float }

name: OpenAI.Chat
comment:
<https://platform.openai.com/docs/api-reference/chat/create>

> Given a chat conversation, the model will return a chat completion response.

@docs create

@docs Input, ChatMessage, ChatMessageRole, ModelID, Output, Choice

aliases.name: ChatMessage
aliases.type: { role : OpenAI.Chat.ChatMessageRole, content : String.String }

aliases.name: Choice
aliases.type: { index : Basics.Int, message : OpenAI.Chat.ChatMessage, finish_reason : Maybe.Maybe String.String }

aliases.name: Input
aliases.comment:
See <https://platform.openai.com/docs/api-reference/chat/create>

If `stream` is set to `True`, response will stream back partial progress. If set, tokens will be sent as data-only server-sent events as they become available, with the stream terminated by a `data: [DONE]` message. This is unsupported in this library for now.
aliases.type: { model : OpenAI.Chat.ModelID, messages : List.List OpenAI.Chat.ChatMessage, temperature : Maybe.Maybe Basics.Float, top_p : Maybe.Maybe Basics.Float, n : Maybe.Maybe Basics.Int, stream : Maybe.Maybe Basics.Bool, stop : Maybe.Maybe (List.List String.String), max_tokens : Maybe.Maybe Basics.Int, presence_penalty : Maybe.Maybe Basics.Float, frequency_penalty : Maybe.Maybe Basics.Float, logit_bias : Maybe.Maybe (Dict.Dict String.String Basics.Int), user : Maybe.Maybe String.String }

aliases.name: Output
aliases.type: { id : String.String, object : String.String, created : Time.Posix, choices : List.List OpenAI.Chat.Choice, usage : OpenAI.Common.Usage }

values.name: create
values.comment:
OpenAI.Chat.create
        { model = GPT3_5_Turbo
        , messages =
            [ ChatMessage SystemRole
                "You are an AI assistant whose goal is to promote the Elm programming language."
            , ChatMessage UserRole
                "What is the best way to learn Elm?"
            ]
        , temperature = Nothing
        , top_p = Nothing
        , n = Nothing
        , stream = Nothing
        , stop = Nothing
        , max_tokens = Nothing
        , presence_penalty = Nothing
        , frequency_penalty = Nothing
        , logit_bias = Nothing
        , user = Nothing
        }
        |> OpenAI.withConfig cfg
        |> Http.task

name: OpenAI.Common
comment:
Common types used in the OpenAI API.

@docs BinaryBlob, DeleteOutput, File, Usage

aliases.type: { contentType : String.String, bytes : Bytes.Bytes }

aliases.name: DeleteOutput
aliases.type: { id : String.String, object : String.String, deleted : Basics.Bool }

aliases.name: File
aliases.type: { id : String.String, object : String.String, bytes : Basics.Int, created_at : Time.Posix, filename : String.String, purpose : String.String }

name: OpenAI.Completion

aliases.name: Choice
aliases.type: { text : String.String, index : Basics.Int, logprobs : Maybe.Maybe OpenAI.Completion.Logprob, finish_reason : Maybe.Maybe String.String }

aliases.name: Input
aliases.comment: If `stream` is set to `True`, partial message deltas will be sent, like in ChatGPT. Tokens will be sent as data-only server-sent events as they become available, with the stream terminated by a `data: [DONE]` message. This is unsupported in this library for now
aliases.type: { model : OpenAI.ModelID.ModelID, prompt : String.String, suffix : Maybe.Maybe String.String, max_tokens : Maybe.Maybe Basics.Int, temperature : Maybe.Maybe Basics.Float, top_p : Maybe.Maybe Basics.Float, n : Maybe.Maybe Basics.Int, stream : Maybe.Maybe Basics.Bool, logprobs : Maybe.Maybe Basics.Int, echo : Maybe.Maybe Basics.Bool, stop : Maybe.Maybe (List.List String.String), presence_penalty : Maybe.Maybe Basics.Float, frequency_penalty : Maybe.Maybe Basics.Float, best_of : Maybe.Maybe Basics.Int, logit_bias : Maybe.Maybe (Dict.Dict String.String Basics.Int), user : Maybe.Maybe String.String }

aliases.name: Logprob
aliases.type: { tokens : List.List String.String, token_logprobs : List.List Basics.Float, top_logprobs : Dict.Dict String.String Basics.Float, text_offset : List.List Basics.Int }

aliases.name: Output
aliases.type: { id : String.String, object : String.String, created : Time.Posix, model : OpenAI.ModelID.ModelID, choices : List.List OpenAI.Completion.Choice, usage : OpenAI.Common.Usage }

values.name: create
values.comment:
<https://beta.openai.com/docs/api-reference/completions/create>

    create
        { model = OpenAI.ModelID.TextDavinci003
        , prompt = "If I were a farmer's son"
        , suffix = Nothing
        , max_tokens = Nothing
        , temperature = Nothing
        , top_p = Nothing
        , n = Nothing
        , stream = Nothing
        , logprobs = Nothing
        , echo = Nothing
        , stop = Nothing
        , presence_penalty = Nothing
        , frequency_penalty = Nothing
        , best_of = Nothing
        , logit_bias = Nothing
        , user = Nothing
        }
        |> OpenAI.withConfig cfg
        |> Http.task
    -- > Task.succeed
    -- >     { choices =
    -- >         [ { finish_reason = "length"
    -- >           , index = 0
    -- >           , logprobs = Nothing
    -- >           , text = "\n\nIf I were a farmer's son
    -- >           , I would rise early each morning"
    -- >           }
    -- >         ]
    -- >     , created = Posix ...
    -- >     , id = "cmpl-..."
    -- >     , model = TextDavinci003
    -- >     , object = "text_completion"
    -- >     , usage =
    -- >         { completion_tokens = 16
    -- >         , prompt_tokens = 7
    -- >         , total_tokens = 23
    -- >         }
    -- >     }

name: OpenAI.Edits

aliases.name: Choice
aliases.type: { text : String.String, index : Basics.Int }

aliases.name: Input
aliases.type: { model : OpenAI.ModelID.ModelID, input : String.String, instruction : String.String, n : Maybe.Maybe Basics.Int, temperature : Maybe.Maybe Basics.Float, top_p : Maybe.Maybe Basics.Float }

aliases.name: Output
aliases.type: { object : String.String, created : Time.Posix, usage : OpenAI.Common.Usage, choices : List.List OpenAI.Edits.Choice }

values.name: create
values.comment:
<https://beta.openai.com/docs/api-reference/edits/create>

    create
        { model = OpenAI.ModelID.TextDavinciEdit001
        , input = "The quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog."
        , instruction = "Make it more formal."
        , n = Just 2
        , temperature = Nothing
        , top_p = Nothing
        }
        |> OpenAI.withConfig cfg
        |> Http.task
    -- > Task.succeed
    -- >     { choices =
    -- >         [ { index = 0, text = "The quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog.\nOne day she was on the roof\nLooking for one thing that the the chicken stole.\nIt was long green and has a big nose.\n" }
    -- >         , { index = 1, text = "The quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog. I am going to Germany tonight.\n" }
    -- >         ]
    -- >     , created = Posix ...
    -- >     , object = "edit"
    -- >     , usage = { completion_tokens = 79, prompt_tokens = 27, total_tokens = 106 }
    -- >     }

name: OpenAI.Embeddings
comment:
> OpenAI’s text embeddings measure the relatedness of text strings. Embeddings are most commonly used for:
>
>   - Search (where results are ranked by relevance to a query string)
>   - Clustering (where text strings are grouped by similarity)
>   - Recommendations (where items with related text strings are recommended)
>   - Anomaly detection (where outliers with little relatedness are identified)
>   - Diversity measurement (where similarity distributions are analyzed)
>   - Classification (where text strings are classified by their most similar label)
>
> An embedding is a vector (list) of floating point numbers. The distance between two vectors measures their relatedness. Small distances suggest high relatedness and large distances suggest low relatedness.

See <https://beta.openai.com/docs/guides/embeddings>

@docs create

@docs Input, Output, Data, Usage

aliases.name: Data
aliases.type: { object : String.String, index : Basics.Int, embedding : List.List Basics.Float }

aliases.name: Input
aliases.type: { model : OpenAI.ModelID.ModelID, input : String.String, user : Maybe.Maybe String.String }

aliases.name: Output
aliases.type: { object : String.String, data : List.List OpenAI.Embeddings.Data, model : OpenAI.ModelID.ModelID, usage : OpenAI.Embeddings.Usage }

aliases.name: Usage
aliases.type: { prompt_tokens : Basics.Int, total_tokens : Basics.Int }

values.name: create
values.comment:
<https://beta.openai.com/docs/api-reference/embeddings/create>

    create
        { model = OpenAI.ModelID.TextEmbeddingAda002
        , input = "The food was delicious and the waiter..."
        , user = Nothing
        }
        |> OpenAI.withConfig cfg
        |> Http.task
    -- > Task.succeed
    -- >     { data =
    -- >         [ { embedding = [0.0023064255,-0.009327292,...,-0.0028842222 ]
    -- >           , index = 0
    -- >           , object = "embedding"
    -- >           }
    -- >         ]
    -- >     , model = Custom "text-embedding-ada-002-v2"
    -- >     , object = "list"
    -- >     , usage = { prompt_tokens = 8, total_tokens = 8 }
    -- >     }

name: OpenAI.File

aliases.name: PromptCompletion
aliases.type: { prompt : String.String, completion : String.String }

name: OpenAI.FineTune
comment:
> Fine-tuning lets you get more out of the models available through the API by providing:
>
>   - Higher quality results than prompt design
>   - Ability to train on more examples than can fit in a prompt
>   - Token savings due to shorter prompts
>   - Lower latency requests

See <https://beta.openai.com/docs/guides/fine-tuning>

@docs create, get, list, listEvents, cancel, delete

@docs Input, BaseModel, FineTune, HyperParameters, JobStatus, Event

unions.name: BaseModel
unions.comment: The name of the base model to fine-tune. You can select one of "ada", "babbage", "curie", "davinci", or a fine-tuned model created after 2022-04-21. To learn more about these models, see the Models documentation.

aliases.name: Event
aliases.type: { object : String.String, created_at : Time.Posix, level : String.String, message : String.String }

aliases.name: FineTune
aliases.type: { id : String.String, object : String.String, model : OpenAI.ModelID.ModelID, created_at : Time.Posix, events : List.List OpenAI.FineTune.Event, fine_tuned_model : Maybe.Maybe String.String, hyperparams : OpenAI.FineTune.HyperParameters, organization_id : String.String, result_files : List.List OpenAI.Common.File, status : OpenAI.FineTune.JobStatus, validation_files : List.List OpenAI.Common.File, training_files : List.List OpenAI.Common.File, updated_at : Time.Posix }

aliases.name: HyperParameters
aliases.type: { batch_size : Basics.Int, learning_rate_multiplier : Basics.Float, n_epochs : Basics.Int, prompt_loss_weight : Basics.Float }

aliases.name: Input
aliases.type: { training_file_id : String.String, validation_file : Maybe.Maybe String.String, model : Maybe.Maybe OpenAI.FineTune.BaseModel, n_epochs : Maybe.Maybe Basics.Int, batch_size : Maybe.Maybe Basics.Int, learning_rate_multiplier : Maybe.Maybe Basics.Float, prompt_loss_weight : Maybe.Maybe Basics.Float, compute_classification_metrics : Maybe.Maybe Basics.Bool, classification_n_classes : Maybe.Maybe Basics.Int, classification_positive_class : Maybe.Maybe String.String, classification_betas : Maybe.Maybe (List.List Basics.Float), suffix : Maybe.Maybe String.String }

name: OpenAI.Image
comment:
> The Images API provides three methods for interacting with images:
>
>   - Creating images from scratch based on a text prompt
>   - Creating edits of an existing image based on a new text prompt
>   - Creating variations of an existing image

See <https://beta.openai.com/docs/guides/images>

@docs create, createEdit, createVariation

@docs Input, EditsInput, ResponseFormat, Size, Output

aliases.name: EditsInput
aliases.type: { image : OpenAI.Common.BinaryBlob, mask : Maybe.Maybe OpenAI.Common.BinaryBlob, prompt : String.String, n : Maybe.Maybe Basics.Int, size : Maybe.Maybe OpenAI.Image.Size, response_format : Maybe.Maybe OpenAI.Image.ResponseFormat, user : Maybe.Maybe String.String }

aliases.name: Input
aliases.type: { prompt : String.String, n : Maybe.Maybe Basics.Int, size : Maybe.Maybe OpenAI.Image.Size, response_format : Maybe.Maybe OpenAI.Image.ResponseFormat, user : Maybe.Maybe String.String }

name: OpenAI.Model

aliases.name: Model
aliases.comment:
> You can refer to the Models documentation to understand what models are available and the differences between them.

<https://platform.openai.com/docs/models>
aliases.type: { id : String.String, object : String.String, owned_by : String.String, permission : List.List OpenAI.Model.Permission }

aliases.name: Permission
aliases.type: { id : String.String, object : String.String, created : Time.Posix, allow_create_engine : Basics.Bool, allow_sampling : Basics.Bool, allow_logprobs : Basics.Bool, allow_search_indices : Basics.Bool, allow_view : Basics.Bool, allow_fine_tuning : Basics.Bool, organization : String.String, group : Maybe.Maybe String.String, is_blocking : Basics.Bool }

values.name: getModel
values.comment:
<https://beta.openai.com/docs/api-reference/models/retrieve>

Retrieves a model instance, providing basic information about the model such as the owner and permissioning.

values.name: listModels
values.comment:
<https://beta.openai.com/docs/api-reference/models/list>

Lists the currently available models, and provides basic information about each one such as the owner and availability.

unions.name: ModelID
unions.comment:
Autogenerated custom type defining all the `model` values that can be used in the API.

DO NOT edit this file by hand; execute `make cli` instead.

values.name: modelIDFromString
values.comment: converts from String; useful for decoding JSON

values.name: stringFromModelID
values.comment: converts to String; useful for encoding in JSON

name: OpenAI.Moderation

aliases.name: Category
aliases.type: { hate : Basics.Bool, hate_threatening : Basics.Bool, self_harm : Basics.Bool, sexual : Basics.Bool, sexual_minors : Basics.Bool, violence : Basics.Bool, violence_graphic : Basics.Bool }

aliases.name: CategoryScore
aliases.type: { hate : Basics.Float, hate_threatening : Basics.Float, self_harm : Basics.Float, sexual : Basics.Float, sexual_minors : Basics.Float, violence : Basics.Float, violence_graphic : Basics.Float }

aliases.name: Input
aliases.type: { input : String.String, model : Maybe.Maybe OpenAI.Moderation.Model }

aliases.name: Moderation
aliases.type: { categories : OpenAI.Moderation.Category, category_scores : OpenAI.Moderation.CategoryScore, flagged : Basics.Bool }

aliases.name: Output
aliases.type: { id : String.String, model : OpenAI.ModelID.ModelID, results : List.List OpenAI.Moderation.Moderation }

values.name: create
values.comment:
<https://beta.openai.com/docs/api-reference/moderations/create>

    create
        { input = "I ** you"
        , model = Just TextModerationLatest
        }
        |> OpenAI.withConfig cfg
        |> Http.task
    -- > Task.succeed
    -- >     { id = "modr-6a5SyUXa0D954a3h9hHi737O8vtM3"
    -- >     , model = Custom "text-moderation-004"
    -- >     , results =
    -- >         [ { categories = { hate = False, hate_threatening = False, self_harm = False, sexual = False, sexual_minors = False, violence = False, violence_graphic = False }
    -- >           , category_scores = { hate = 0.000005618692284770077, hate_threatening = 7.34394856038989e-9, self_harm = 1.334657184770549e-7, sexual = 0.001665698830038309, sexual_minors = 7.969669013618841e-7, violence = 0.000050713490054477006, violence_graphic = 4.190181073226995e-7 }
    -- >           , flagged = False
    -- >           }
    -- >         ]
    -- >     }
